prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
# Translation of "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."

## French
"Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n'importe quelle dispute n'aurait pu le faire."

*Note: The French adds "le faire" (to do it) at the end to complete the sense of what the argument "could have" done.*

## Japanese
"私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな口論よりも多くを語っていた。"
(Watashitachi no aida no chinmoku wa, donna kōron yori mo ōku wo katatte ita.)

*Note: The Japanese phrase "多くを語る" (ōku wo kataru) literally means "to speak many things" but is used idiomatically to convey "to tell/say a lot" or "to be revealing."*

## Arabic
"الصمت بيننا قال أكثر مما كان يمكن لأي جدال أن يقوله."
(Assamt baynana qala akthara mimma kana yumkin li'ay jidal an yaqulahu.)

*Note: Arabic required adding "يقوله" (yaqulahu - "to say it") at the end to complete the comparison of what the argument "could have" said, making the implied object explicit.*
